description.s: Hobson's item graph can contain cycles (item A attaches B, B attaches A). Rather than forbidding cycles at the data level, the system permits them and provides safe primitives for traversal and rendering.
— knowledge graphs, bidirectional relationships, and search results naturally create cycles
— the kernel detects cycles during render dispatch
— how to display a cycle is context-dependent
— API design forces acknowledgment of cycle cases
The kernel tracks which items are currently being rendered. If
is called for an item already in the render path, it's a cycle.
Views that render attachments should provide an
Views that don't render attachments (simple note views, code views) don't need
— check if an item's descendant graph contains cycles
These are advisory — they don't prevent the operation, they inform the caller.
All cycle-safe traversal follows the same core pattern:
— Cycle detection in the type hierarchy
